operator_request: Activate WP-FF-003 and Phase 0, create and remotely checkpoint all later Phase 0 packets as detailed documentation-linked stubs first, then create a clean deterministic third sibling folder for Cargo builds and tests and begin implementing; sub-agent assistance is authorized with primary-agent proof review.

spec_anchors.scope: accepted physical topology, xtask location, dependency direction, policy ownership, and architecture-check proof classes
canonical commands, applicable PR gate, reports, and tool limitations
toolchain, lint, unsafe, dependency, review, and change-evidence requirements
first product closure and no-context manual stub
bootstrap authorization and claims that remain blocked
machine-readable policies, canonical reports, change evidence, and exception separation
FF-BUILD-036 through FF-BUILD-067 and named verification gates

preserve: Rust-only product direction and FFmpeg/ffprobe supervised external-process boundary
accepted coarse topology without adding shipped crates
change: Turn the currently unimplemented architecture and applicable PR gates into executable commands
Create the minimal locked build/ Cargo and xtask substrate plus the sole root rust-toolchain selector
Create machine-readable policy, proof mapping, reports, and negative fixtures
Create a no-context Phase 0 operating manual
non_goals: Compatibility, dependency-selection, durability, or performance conclusions
New runtime or native dependency approval

assumptions: The first compiled workspace member is non-shipped fforager-xtask under build/tools/fforager-xtask.
The repository has three owned roots: .GOV/ governance, product/ shipped runtime code/assets and product-local tests, and build/ Cargo workspace/shared test infrastructure.
The sole root rust-toolchain.toml is a repository bootstrap selector rather than a fourth code/config root.
The bootstrap can use Rust std plus pinned built-in Cargo toolchain components unless research proves a small non-runtime development dependency is necessary and policy-compliant.
Future gates extend a stable report envelope but cannot reinterpret a failed or unimplemented check as PASS.
The governance checkpoint containing packet activation and detailed stubs must be remotely verified before product files are created.

sources.evidence: Virtual workspaces share one root lockfile, require an explicit resolver, and support inherited package and lint policy.
Rust 1.97.1 is the current patch release and fixes an LLVM miscompilation in 1.97.0.
rustup selects a toolchain override by current-directory ancestry, not Cargo --manifest-path; minimal profiles can add rustfmt and Clippy components.
Cargo metadata format-version 1 and Cargo JSON messages are the stable external-tool surfaces for declared/resolved graph, target, build-script, artifact, and native-link evidence.
A repository-local Rust xtask binary is a field-used cross-platform orchestration pattern; child tools should use direct process argument vectors.
Cargo/rustc provide typed JSON/JSONL tool evidence; SARIF is useful for static-analysis findings but is not a complete orchestration receipt envelope.
cargo-deny 0.20.2 is the current pinned candidate for advisory, ban/duplicate, license, source, and build-script policy evidence, with documented proof limitations.
Mature negative-fixture precedent uses checked-in minimal fixture workspaces and exact expected diagnostics.
cargo package --locked provides a pristine package listing and verification build useful for later shipped-artifact smoke tests.
GitHub documents full commit SHAs as immutable third-party Action references for CI supply-chain pinning.

patterns_found: Use one virtual workspace with explicit members, resolver 3, inherited lint/package policy, and a tracked root Cargo.lock.

Use Cargo CLI JSON rather than unstable Cargo internals or human cargo-tree text; inspect both declared dependency rows and the resolved graph so optional, target, build, dev, and renamed edges are not omitted.

Keep proof classes separate: metadata for graph facts, conservative source/build-script checks for declared source boundaries, Cargo build JSON for artifacts/native links, and runtime/package tests for behavioral claims.
Use a versioned custom JSON gate envelope with exact commands, exit states, trigger/rule IDs, findings, fixture expectations, skips with authority, proof limitations, artifacts, and hashes.
Each negative fixture must contain one intended violation and must fail with the exact stable diagnostic code; wrong-reason failure is a validator failure.
A zero-shipped-member clean-artifact check is explicit NOT_APPLICABLE in WP-003, not a vacuous PASS; later shipped members use a fresh directory with .GOV absent.
Aggregate PASS is legal only when every required applicable child check passes; NOT_RUN, NOT_IMPLEMENTED, unknown triggers, and unauthorized skips cannot become PASS.
reuse_opportunities: Reuse Cargo metadata and compiler JSON instead of building a custom dependency resolver.
Reuse the active packet change-evidence surface instead of a second editable receipt.
Reuse Rust std and direct std::process::Command calls for the initial xtask rather than adding a runtime dependency.
Reuse cargo-deny as the later exact-version dependency-policy implementation instead of duplicating cargo-audit at bootstrap.
Reuse one report envelope and stable diagnostic vocabulary for every later Phase 0 and release gate.
rejected_options: A product-local rust-toolchain.toml alone, because rustup will not select it for the canonical repository-root Cargo invocation.
Changing the canonical command to cargo +<version> or changing its working directory without an authority decision.
Using Cargo as a library or parsing cargo tree text, because those are unstable or lossy compared with official JSON surfaces.
Treating Cargo metadata, Cargo links, lexical grep, cargo-deny, Clippy, or any single tool as complete runtime/native/security proof.
Using SARIF as the sole wrapper report format.
Running cargo-audit and cargo-deny as duplicate bootstrap advisory gates.
Running clean-artifact smoke inside the repository where ambient governance files could mask a runtime dependency.
research.selected_approach: Create a resolver-3 virtual workspace at build/Cargo.toml with only non-shipped build/tools/fforager-xtask; keep shipped code and product-local tests under product/; keep shared fixtures, cross-package test harnesses, fuzzing, benchmarks, reports, and target output under build/; pin Rust 1.97.1 minimal plus rustfmt and Clippy through the sole root rust-toolchain.toml; use Cargo metadata and compiler JSON through direct argument vectors; default-deny undeclared members, wrong-root files, edges, rules, fixtures, tools, exceptions, and report triggers; use exact diagnostic-coded fixtures; emit collision-safe atomic versioned JSON reports; make zero shipped artifacts explicit NOT_APPLICABLE; and pin dependency-policy tooling without auto-installing it.
risks: Wrong-root build/test files or duplicate toolchain selectors can recreate ownership drift and make models run inconsistent gates.
Optional, target, build, dev, or renamed edges can be omitted by a resolved-graph-only checker.
Machine-local absolute paths can make reports non-portable or nondeterministic.
Build scripts, proc macros, downloaded/prebuilt artifacts, and native links can evade metadata-only validation.
Mutable advisory data can make two nominally identical dependency reports incomparable.
Fixtures can drift or fail before reaching the intended validator.
Parallel report writers can overwrite evidence.
A clean-artifact check can falsely pass if the test retains repository ancestry or silently treats zero shipped artifacts as success.
mitigations: Apply the Operator-selected three-root split, keep one root rust-toolchain.toml selector, validate exact path ownership, and reject duplicate selectors.
Inspect declared and resolved Cargo dependency facts and add exact fixtures for every dependency kind and target condition.
Normalize all reported paths repository-relative and test Windows and Linux path behavior.
Default-deny build/proc-macro/native behavior, combine policy/source/build-JSON/artifact checks, and report residual proof limits.
Record advisory database identity and freshness and fail stale/offline evidence rather than silently reuse it.
Require one intended mutation, an exact diagnostic code, a positive control, and completeness checks across every REQUIRED architecture rule.
Use unique report IDs, create-new temporary files, atomic rename, and content hashes.
Enumerate shipped=true members from policy; emit NOT_APPLICABLE for the WP-003 zero-member case and later stage outside the repository with .GOV absent.
validation_plan: Run every negative fixture RED and require its exact stable diagnostic code.
Run the real bootstrap architecture-check GREEN with a versioned report.
Run locked format, check, Clippy, tests, doctests, and docs on the pinned toolchain.
Run dependency-policy evidence with exact tool and advisory database identity when the external tool is provisioned.
Require explicit clean-artifact NOT_APPLICABLE while no shipped member exists.
Run verify-pr and prove aggregate status rules through negative mutations.
Repeat architecture and PR gates on Windows and Linux before treating cross-platform path/process behavior as proven.
Independently mutate members, every edge class, build scripts, proc macros, unsafe policy, .GOV runtime reads, exception references, and report triggers and confirm fail-closed behavior.

authority_decision.decision: Use .GOV/ for governance, product/ for shipped runtime code/assets and product-local tests, build/ for Cargo workspace/shared build-test infrastructure, and one root rust-toolchain.toml selector.
authority_decision.basis: Cargo supports explicitly listed sibling workspace members through package.workspace, shares the workspace lockfile and target directory under build/, and rustup requires the selector in repository-root ancestry for canonical root commands.
rejected_fallbacks: Product-only Cargo workspace with build/ used merely as target output
Separate product and build workspaces with two lockfiles and policy graphs

attack_surfaces: Unknown rules, fields, fixtures, members, or dependencies silently ignored
A fixture failing before it reaches the intended validator
A validator checking Cargo metadata but overclaiming runtime, package, or artifact behavior
verify-pr converting NOT_IMPLEMENTED, NOT_RUN, or skipped checks into PASS
Product or clean-artifact execution reading .GOV or build/
Cargo/test infrastructure placed under product/ or governance placed under build/
minimum_controls: Mutation fixtures with exact failure identifiers
Aggregate PASS only when every required applicable child check passes
Three-root placement checks and duplicate-selector rejection
Clean copy verification with .GOV and build/ absent from shipped runtime ancestry
Pinned toolchain and exact tool preflight without auto-install

execution_decomposition: Use the verified remote packet/stub checkpoint
Complete current research and select the smallest field-aligned bootstrap design
Update authority, topology, design, registry, and stubs for the Operator-selected three-root layout
Create the root toolchain selector and locked non-shipped build/ Cargo/xtask substrate and policies
Implement architecture-check, exact negative fixtures, and versioned reports
Implement bootstrap-applicable verify-pr and clean-artifact smoke
Write the no-context model manual stub
Run complete positive, negative, adversarial, and simplification verification
